dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 7445 IH: Evergreen Community Safety Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Ms. Pettersen introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on the Judiciary

form.official-title: To amend title 18, United States Code, to provide special rules for the time period for complying court orders or warrants to disclose information for certain providers, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Evergreen Community Safety Act of 2026.

section.header: Time period for complying with a court order or warrant for certain providers
section.text: Section 2703 of title 18, United States Code, is amended by adding at the end the following:

paragraph.text: A warrant or a court order for disclosure issued under this section shall require that a covered provider disclose the contents of a wire or electronic communication, or the records or other information sought not later than 72 hours after the issuance of the warrant or court order.
A court may extend the time period under paragraph (1) by periods of not more than 7 days if the information sought is voluminous or complex.
The time period for a covered provider to file a motion under subsection (g)(2) is 48 hours.

Notwithstanding any other provision of law, including subsection (e), an individual who is harmed by the failure of a covered provider to comply with a warrant or court order for disclosure in the time period provided by the court may bring a civil action in an appropriate district court of the United States seeking injunctive relief and damages.
The term covered provider means a provider of electronic communication service or remote computing service that has 1,000,000 or more users, subscribers, or customers.
